title: Utility Fundamentals
content:
### Utility Class Pattern

Tailwind follows a consistent naming convention: `{property}-{value}`

```html
<!-- Property-value pattern -->
<div class="bg-blue-500">        <!-- background-color: blue-500 -->
<div class="text-white">          <!-- color: white -->
<div class="p-4">                  <!-- padding: 1rem -->
<div class="font-bold">            <!-- font-weight: bold -->
```

### Reading Tailwind Docs

Each utility page shows:
- Class name and its CSS equivalent
- All available values
- Responsive and state variants
- Examples

### Combining Utilities

Build complex styles by stacking multiple utilities:

```html
<button class="
  bg-indigo-600           /* background */
  text-white              /* text color */
  font-semibold           /* font weight */
  py-2 px-6               /* padding y, padding x */
  rounded-lg              /* border radius */
  shadow-md               /* box shadow */
  hover:bg-indigo-700     /* hover state */
  focus:outline-none      /* focus state */
  focus:ring-2            /* focus ring */
  focus:ring-indigo-400   /* focus ring color */
">
  Save Changes
</button>
```

### Responsive Prefixes

Add responsive breakpoints before the utility:

```html
<div class="
  w-full          /* mobile: full width */
  md:w-1/2       /* tablet: half width */
  lg:w-1/3       /* desktop: one third */
">
```

Breakpoints: `sm:`, `md:`, `lg:`, `xl:`, `2xl:`

### State Variants

Style elements based on their state:

```html
<button class="
  bg-gray-500
  hover:bg-gray-600       /* on hover */
  focus:bg-gray-600       /* on focus */
  active:bg-gray-700      /* on click */
  disabled:opacity-50     /* when disabled */
">
  Submit
</button>
```
codeExample:
Case Study

Combining utilities creates rich, consistent designs.
